result.collection: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors (baseline confirmed with all docstrings in place; identical to target 16738/11). py_compile OK on all 21 modified src files.

codex_points: CODEX (GPT-5.6, medium effort) reversed its prior DOCS-REVISE and now gates the Phase 3A docstring batch as DOCS-SAFE.

The three checks are treated as jointly conclusive: docstring-stripped AST identical to HEAD limits the delta to docstrings only; oracle replay is byte-identical (sha 9c0a3d43...) confirming exercised runtime behavior is unchanged; and the per-symbol __doc__-consumer audit closes the last functional channel (62 kept, 0 load-bearing; load-bearing ones would have been reverted).
The former hole (argparse/pydantic/doctest/inspect.getdoc could make __doc__ load-bearing) is now considered closed by the audit finding no load-bearing docstring among the kept 62.
CODEX notes that help(), pydoc, and inspect.getdoc() will intentionally surface the revised text, but this is expected documentation exposure, not a runtime behavior dependency.
The only residual it flags is out of scope: unknown external code arbitrarily branching on __doc__ is not an in-scope behavioral contract, so it does not block shipping.
Verdict: provably a zero-behavior-change docs batch, safe to ship.
